issues.title: arbitrary-send-eth
issues.description:
Escrow.releaseFunds(address,address) (contract.sol#48-67) sends eth to arbitrary user
	Dangerous calls:
	- (sent,None) = address(buyer).call{value: funds[buyer][seller]}() (contract.sol#59)
issues.recommendation: Add access control and input validation

issues.title: reentrancy-eth
issues.description:
Reentrancy in Escrow.releaseFunds(address,address) (contract.sol#48-67):
	External calls:
	- (sent,None) = address(buyer).call{value: funds[buyer][seller]}() (contract.sol#59)
	State variables written after the call(s):
	- funds[buyer][seller] = 0 (contract.sol#63)
	Escrow.funds (contract.sol#9) can be used in cross function reentrancies:
	- Escrow.approveTransaction(address,address) (contract.sol#33-45)
	- Escrow.deposit(address,address,uint256) (contract.sol#18-30)
	- Escrow.funds (contract.sol#9)
	- Escrow.releaseFunds(address,address) (contract.sol#48-67)

issues.title: reentrancy-events
issues.description:
Reentrancy in Escrow.releaseFunds(address,address) (contract.sol#48-67):
	External calls:
	- (sent,None) = address(buyer).call{value: funds[buyer][seller]}() (contract.sol#59)
	Event emitted after the call(s):
	- FundsReleased(buyer,seller,funds[buyer][seller]) (contract.sol#66)
issues.recommendation: Review and apply security best practices

issues.title: solc-version
issues.description:
Version constraint ^0.8.20 contains known severe issues (https://solidity.readthedocs.io/en/latest/bugs.html)
	- VerbatimInvalidDeduplication
	- FullInlinerNonExpressionSplitArgumentEvaluationOrder
	- MissingSideEffectsOnSelectorAccess.
It is used by:
	- ^0.8.20 (contract.sol#2)
